title: Service Backplane
description: A backend-first starter generated from public service and resilience repositories, designed for operational and platform engineering portfolios.
sourceLabel: Inspired by real-world repositories from the Netflix engineering ecosystem

theme.name: Slate Mono
theme.reason: Quiet monochrome framing for text-first portfolios and resumes.

previewSnapshot.summary: Netflix Open Source Platform The portfolio draft reflects focused most often on Java, Python, JavaScript, and Go and based in Los Gatos, California, with selected work such as void-model, metaflow, and zuul.

subheadline.value: Built from 6 featured repositories drawn from recent public GitHub work with a focus on Java, Python, and JavaScript. This draft starts from real code, repository context, and README signals instead of placeholder portfolio copy.

description.value: Netflix Open Source Platform Based in Los Gatos, California. Selected repositories and README content are being used to shape the project story and technical focus.
Share the clearest way to connect for roles, freelance work, collaborations, or portfolio conversations.
Add the professional destinations you want visitors to open next, including your resume, cover letter, LinkedIn, Handshake, and portfolio links.

repositories.name: Netflix/Hystrix
repositories.description: Hystrix is a latency and fault tolerance library designed to isolate points of access to remote systems, services and 3rd party libraries, stop cascading failure and enable resilience in complex distributed systems where failure is inevitable.
repositories.readmeExcerpt: Hystrix: Latency and Fault Tolerance for Distributed Systems

repositories.name: Netflix/chaosmonkey
repositories.description: Chaos Monkey is a resiliency tool that helps applications tolerate random instance failures.

repositories.name: Netflix/zuul
repositories.description: Zuul is a gateway service that provides dynamic routing, monitoring, resiliency, security, and more.
repositories.readmeExcerpt: Zuul is an L7 application gateway that provides capabilities for dynamic routing, monitoring, resiliency, security, and more. Please view the wiki for usage, information, HOWTO, etc https://github.com/Netflix/zuul/wiki

repositories.name: Netflix/eureka
repositories.description: AWS Service registry for resilient mid-tier load balancing and failover.
repositories.readmeExcerpt: Eureka is a RESTful (Representational State Transfer) service that is primarily used in the AWS cloud for the purpose of discovery, load balancing and failover of middle-tier servers. It plays a critical role in Netflix mid-tier infra.

repositories.name: Netflix/falcor
repositories.description: A JavaScript library for efficient data fetching
repositories.readmeExcerpt: **2.0** is the current stable Falcor release. **0.x** and **1.x** users are welcome to upgrade.

repositories.name: Netflix/metaflow
repositories.description: Build, Manage and Deploy AI/ML Systems
repositories.readmeExcerpt: Metaflow is a human-centric framework designed to help scientists and engineers **build and manage real-life AI and ML systems**. Serving teams of all sizes and scale, Metaflow streamlines the entire development lifecycle—from rapid prototyping in notebooks to reliable, maintainable production deployments—enabling teams to iterate quickly and deliver robust systems efficiently.

hero.headline: Netflix Open Source Platform

about.title: About this backend starter
about.description: Netflix Open Source Platform Based in Los Gatos, California. Selected repositories and README content are being used to shape the project story and technical focus. Featured work is drawn from repositories like Netflix/Hystrix, Netflix/chaosmonkey, and Netflix/zuul.

professional.availability: Designed for portfolios centered on service architecture, reliability, and platform ownership.
